blocks.headline: Espresso, six ways. Filter, three. A short list of pastries from down the street.
blocks.subheadline: We don't carry flavored syrups. Oat, almond, and house cashew milk are no extra. If you want it the way your grandmother made it, ask — we probably can.

items.title: Espresso
items.description: $3.50 — Double, 18g in / 36g out, 28-second pull. The benchmark.

items.title: Macchiato
items.description: $3.75 — Espresso with a thumb of foamed milk. Italian, not the syrup kind.

items.title: Cortado
items.description: $4.25 — Equal parts espresso and warm milk, served in a 4oz glass.

items.title: Flat White
items.description: $4.75 — Double ristretto, microfoam, 6oz. Our most ordered drink.

items.title: Latte
items.description: $5.00 — Double espresso, steamed milk, 8oz. Add a quarter shot for a stronger cup, no charge.

items.title: Mocha
items.description: $5.50 — Espresso, steamed milk, 70% Valrhona ganache made fresh on Sundays.

items.title: V60
items.description: $5.00 — 22g dose, 360g water, four-minute brew. Bright, clean, the cup we recommend first.

items.title: Chemex
items.description: $11 (for two) — 42g dose, 700g water, six-minute brew. Built to share.

items.title: Aeropress
items.description: $5.00 — Inverted method, 17g dose, two-minute steep. Sweet, full-bodied, surprisingly forgiving.

items.title: Banana bread
items.description: $4.00 — by the slice, made with brown butter.

blocks.text: Oat, almond, and house-made cashew milk are no extra charge. We don't do flavored syrups. We do, however, have a very serious hot chocolate in winter.

blocks.title: The bar opens at 7am.
blocks.description: Tuesday through Sunday. Closed Mondays. Saturday mornings before nine are the slowest and the best.
